topic: Area of Study 4: Western Classical Tradition since 1910
subject: music

items.correct: A recurring main theme (A) alternates with contrasting episodes (B, C, etc.).
items.misconception: A round where voices enter at different times.
items.why: Students confuse rondo (alternating refrain) with round (canon). Rondo: ABACA, ABACABA, etc. The A theme keeps coming back, like a refrain.

items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.

items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.
Correct answer: Beethoven. “Mozart” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: 104. “41” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The real figure is 9 symphonies. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
